app.name: BranchBoard
app.tagline: One task. One branch. One workflow.

release.title: New BranchBoard version: SQL, sync, and admin announcements
release.message: Please read this short summary before updating. BranchBoard data is being moved from one large JSON record into SQLite tables, so the board should stay faster and more stable as the number of tasks grows. We are also enabling better sync between users and visible admin announcements, so everyone knows when a new version should be installed.
release.changes: Existing data is migrated into SQLite tables without losing current work. The board should perform better with many tasks, comments, and subtasks. Changes are synchronized more reliably between users connected to the same database. Admins can show a clear update alert with a download link.
release.tutorial: How to use it: download and install the new BranchBoard version, open the board and wait for the migration, read the admin announcement, and ideally update all workstations on the same day.

quickAdd.title: New task
quickAdd.requiredHint: A title is required to save the task.

columnConfig.title: Column configuration
columnConfig.noCommands: No commands yet. Add the first one.
columnConfig.label: Label (e.g. Run tests)
columnConfig.notAllowed: Command is not on the allowlist (branchBoard.allowedCommands).
columnConfig.securityNote: Commands run without a shell, allowed binaries only. Variables: {{branch}} {{taskId}} {{slug}} {{baseBranch}} {{targetBranch}} {{mainBranch}}. Every run is recorded in .branchboard/audit.log.

automation.title: Built-in Git automation for this column
automation.customNote: The commands below (onEnter/onLeave) are extra, custom steps (e.g. lint, tests) — they run alongside the Git automation described above, independently of it.
automation.disabledWarning: Automatic Git actions on move are disabled in settings (branchBoard.runGitActionsOnMove). Enable that setting for the description above to actually run.
automation.aiAgent: When a task enters this column: BranchBoard requires a selected agent and prompt. The agent may prepare local changes on an AI branch, without push, merge or deploy.
automation.feature: When a task enters this column: if it has no branch yet, a new branch with prefix "{prefix}" is created from the current branch; if it already has one, that branch is checked out.
automation.review: When a task enters this column: the task's branch is pushed to {remote} so a Pull Request can be opened.
automation.staging: When a task enters this column: the task's branch is safely merged (--no-ff) into {branch} and pushed to {remote}.
automation.productionMerge: When a task enters this column: the task's branch is pushed, then — after your confirmation — safely merged into {branch} (checkout → pull → merge --no-ff → push) and the task is moved to DONE.
automation.productionNoMerge: When a task enters this column: the task's branch is pushed, but merging into {branch} is DISABLED (branchBoard.allowDirectMergeToMain = false) — the task is moved to the test column without merging into main.
automation.none: This column has no Git stage assigned — no automation will run.

task.finishHint: Push branch and move this task forward safely.
task.copyAiPromptHint: Use this prompt in Cursor, Claude or Copilot Chat.

branchBadge.origin: On origin — visible to the team
branchBadge.pushHint: Push the branch to origin so the team can see it.
branchBadge.checkRulesHint: Runs the configured command (branchBoard.runCommandBeforeFinish) and shows the result below.
branchBadge.summarizeHint: Copies a prompt with the changed files and commits to paste into Cursor/Claude/Copilot.
branchBadge.pasteAiHint: Paste an AI report here (e.g. from a code review) so the reviewer sees it without leaving the task.
branchBadge.noVerifyCommand: No verification command configured (branchBoard.runCommandBeforeFinish) — set it in Settings for this button to actually check anything.
branchBadge.summarizePromptIntro: Summarize the changes on branch {branch} for a reviewer. Be specific and concise.
branchBadge.summarizePromptRules: Check compliance with the project's rules (scope of the change, no unrelated refactors, clean code) and write a short summary ready to paste into this task.
branchBadge.aiNotesPlaceholder: Paste the AI's response here (change summary, code review result, etc.) — saved automatically.

task.noBranchHint: Create a branch from this task to start working.
task.title: Title
task.typeLockedHint: Type drives the branch name. A branch already exists, so the type can't be changed.
task.chatEmptyHint: Send the first message to start the task conversation.
task.branchDiffers: Current branch differs from the task branch.

production.productionServerRollback: On the production server with local SQLite, tasks cannot be moved back from Production. Move them from a local SSH client instead; BranchBoard will switch to the task branch there.
production.productionChecklistIncomplete: A task cannot be moved to Production until all subtasks are done.
production.productionRequiresChecklist: Production is available only after all subtasks are done.
production.checklistLocked: Subtasks are locked in the Production column.

task.noWorkLog: No recorded work on this task yet.

files.title: Changed files (branch)
files.empty: No changes vs main (or the branch doesn't exist locally).

task.acceptancePlaceholder: One criterion per line — when is the task considered done…

help.title: A short, unambiguous task title. Shown on the board card.
help.context: Recent traces of work (commits and events) plus task dates. Lets you see momentum and spot tasks that have stalled.
help.changedFiles: Files changed on the branch vs main. Click a name to open; 'Compare' shows the diff. Fast code review and risk read.
help.details: Fields that describe the task. They feed the Team view, filters, risk and the overdue calculation.
help.assignee: Who owns the task. Used in the Team view and the 'Mine' filter.
help.priority: How important the task is — helps order the work.
help.type: Type of work: feature, bugfix, hotfix, chore, refactor or docs. Determines the branch prefix (e.g. feature/…, bugfix/…).
help.status: Task state: open, in progress, done.
help.column: The board column = a workflow stage (e.g. review, testing). Metrics and risk are derived from it.
help.dueDate: Due date. Drives the 'overdue by N days' counter.
help.description: Context for the team and the AI agent. A better description means a better AI prompt and lower risk.
help.acceptance: Conditions that must hold for the task to be done. One per line — fed into the AI prompt. When empty, unchecked checklist items are used.
help.git: Operations on the task branch: create, switch, push and safe finish.
help.branch: The branch linked to this task. Rule of thumb: one task, one branch.
help.suggest: Insert a suggested branch name based on the task title.
help.deploy: Deploy the branch to DEV and mark testing. Visible in Command Center → Deployments.
help.safety: Safe copies (backup/tag) and rollback commands. Nothing destructive runs automatically.
help.ai: A ready-made agent prompt and a review checklist for AI work.
help.aiAssisted: Tick if the task was done with an AI agent — it then appears in the AI Review panel.
help.aiModel: Which model/agent did the work (e.g. Claude, GPT-4).
help.aiChecklist: A checklist to verify AI-generated changes.
help.checklist: Sub-tasks. They also build the acceptance criteria in the AI prompt.

tip.createBranch: Create a branch from this task and switch to it.
tip.checkout: Switch the repository to this task's branch.
tip.checkoutPublic: Switch to this task branch. If it is not local, BranchBoard fetches it from {remote}; if it has not been pushed publicly, you will see a message.
tip.push: Push the branch to the remote.
tip.copyBranch: Copy the branch name to the clipboard.
tip.updateFromMain: Switch to this task's branch and align it with origin/main: fetches the latest changes from the server and updates the branch. If there is a conflict, the operation is safely aborted and BranchBoard shows the list of files that need manual merging.
tip.merge: Merge the branch into main (with confirmation and backup).
tip.deployDev: Run the DEV deploy command for this branch.
tip.openDev: Open the DEV environment URL in the browser.
tip.markTested: Mark the DEV deployment as tested.
tip.backup: Create a backup branch as a safe copy before a risky operation.
tip.tag: Create a safety tag at the current main.
tip.copyRollback: Copy ready-made rollback commands to the clipboard.
tip.revert: Safely revert the last commit (creates a new commit, no history rewrite).
tip.revertFromOrigin: Revert the last commit and push the result to origin — e.g. after an unwanted deploy. If the branch has a connected webhook (CI/CD), it fires normally, just like for any other push, so the deployment rolls back to the previous version. This is NOT a force-push or reset — history is never rewritten.
tip.guide: Open the Git documentation on undoing changes.

deploy.title: Deployments
deploy.configureHint: Set branchBoard.devDeployCommand to enable DEV deploys.

safety.title: Safety
safety.backupOn: A backup branch will be created before merge.
safety.backupOff: Backup before merge is disabled (enable it in settings).
safety.note: Rollback commands are only copied — destructive operations are never run automatically.

branchFlow.subtitle: An operational center for managing branches as tasks.

garden.intro: Your project as a tree: main is the trunk, and each branch is work growing from it. Expand a branch to see its commits (leaves). Merge healthy branches, prune the withered ones.
garden.trunk: trunk — finished, reviewed changes land here
garden.empty: No branches — the whole tree is just the trunk (main).
garden.emptyHint: Create a branch from a task to start a new limb.
garden.sproutHint: You're on main. Create a branch from a task and the tree will start climbing upward.

help.healthy: The branch is alive: it has a task and fresh commits. Keep going.
help.ready: Mature branch — looks ready to merge into main (with confirmation).
help.stale: No activity for a while. Update from main or clean it up.
help.risk: Elevated risk — check the changed files before merging.
help.backup: A backup/archive branch — a safety copy, usually a cleanup candidate.
help.noTask: A branch with no linked task — link it or create a task.

cleanup.note: Branches that look removable. Nothing is deleted automatically — review and select.

reasonHelp.backup: A backup/archive branch — a safety copy, usually unneeded after a while.
reasonHelp.staleNoTask: No activity for a while and no linked task.
reasonHelp.noCommits: The branch adds nothing vs main.
reasonHelp.localOnlyOld: Exists only locally and hasn't been touched in a long time.

impact.emptyHint: Active branches don't touch the configured project areas.
impact.note: Which project areas active branches touch (based on changed files).

git.noRepo: Open a Git repository to use BranchBoard Git features.
git.noUser: Git user not detected. Configure git config user.name and user.email.
git.dirtyTree: You have uncommitted changes. Commit or stash them before finishing this task.
git.mergeConfirm: This will merge the task branch into main. Continue?
git.mergeConfirmDetail: This checks out main, pulls, merges, and pushes. The task branch will be merged into main.
git.branchNotFound: Branch does not exist. Create it first.
git.mergeConflict: Merge conflict. Operation aborted, the task was not closed.
git.taskNotClosed: The task was NOT closed because a Git operation failed.

aiAgent.title: AI Agent
aiAgent.notConfiguredTitle: AI Agent is not configured for this task.
aiAgent.notConfiguredHint: Pick an agent and model to generate a prompt and run AI work on this branch.
aiAgent.safetyNote: The agent works locally. BranchBoard will not push, merge or deploy without your decision.

cursorPersonas.title: Cursor personas
cursorPersonas.empty: No .cursor/agents/*.md files found in this workspace.

aiAgent.modelHint: This list comes from the agent configuration in BranchBoard settings.
aiAgent.modelDisabled: This agent does not declare model selection.
aiAgent.promptPlaceholder: Click “Generate prompt” to collect the description, checklist, chat, files and project rules.
aiAgent.cardHint: This task was performed or prepared by AI.
aiAgent.costNotConfigured: No pricing configured for this agent — set branchBoard.aiAgents[].pricing in settings to see an estimated cost.

aiCostGuard.title: AI Cost Guard
aiCostGuard.messagePlaceholder: What do you want the agent to do? (optional — defaults to the prompt above)
aiCostGuard.usedLocalModel: The local model advised on this decision.
aiCostGuard.confirmRun: Continue and run Cursor CLI with this context?

onboarding.intro: Create a Git-connected Kanban board for this repository. Tasks can create branches, switch branches and safely finish work without leaving VS Code.

settings.title: Settings
settings.requireCleanTree: Require clean working tree before finish
settings.titleBarEnabledHint: Changes the VS Code / Cursor title bar (top of the window, where the folder name or SSH remote normally shows) for this workspace only.
settings.titleBarShowBranch: Show current branch in the title bar
settings.titleBarShowBranchHint: VS Code's title bar has a single background for the whole text, so the branch name can't have its own colored badge — it's shown after a separator in a muted tone instead.
settings.branchButtonHint: VS Code/Cursor give extensions no public API to add a custom-colored button inside the native top title bar itself (the area where, e.g., Cursor's own "Agents Window" button lives) — that's proprietary editor chrome. The closest real, extensible equivalent is a clickable button in the status bar (bottom of the window) showing the current branch. Clicking it opens the task branch switcher.
settings.branchButtonEnabled: Show branch button in the status bar
settings.sshHint: Server mode uses a shared SQLite database reached over SSH. Settings are saved to this project's .vscode/settings.json.
settings.sshHostHint: Leave empty when the database lives on this machine (opened locally).
settings.connActiveServer: Connected to the server — data syncs with the shared SQLite database.
settings.connFallback: Server mode is set, but you are running LOCALLY (connection failed). Data is NOT synced with the server.
settings.connLocal: Local mode — data in a JSON file in the workspace.
settings.aiAgentsModelsHint: Refresh the model list from the agent's CLI (if supported), enter prices, and turn individual models on/off.
settings.aiAgentsNoAgents: No AI agents configured — add them in branchBoard.aiAgents.
settings.aiAgentsNoListCommand: This agent has no model-listing command configured (listModelsArgs).
settings.aiAgentsNoModels: No known models — refresh from the CLI or add them in branchBoard.aiAgents (models).

today.title: Today's Tasks
today.subtitle: Your work list for today — overdue, today's, and upcoming tasks in one place.

section.noDateHint: Open tasks without a due date, sorted by priority.

empty.title: No tasks in this range
empty.hint: Nice! Nothing overdue or scheduled for this period.
empty.noDate: No tasks without a due date

branchMap.title: Branch Map
branchMap.noData: No map data. Open a Git repository.
branchMap.subtitle: The real flow of work: branches, tasks, commits, risk and merge readiness.

currentBranch.title: Current branch
currentBranch.subtitle: Everything BranchBoard knows about the current work and its linked task.
currentBranch.noTaskTitle: This branch is not linked to a BranchBoard task yet.
currentBranch.noTaskDescription: You can create a task from the current branch or link this branch to an existing task.
currentBranch.linkPick: Pick a task without a branch…
currentBranch.onMainTitle: You are on the main branch.
currentBranch.onMainDescription: The main branch should contain finished and reviewed changes. Create a separate branch to work on a task.
currentBranch.actions: What to do with this branch?
currentBranch.dirtyTreeWarning: You have local changes that have not been committed yet.
currentBranch.noCommits: This branch has no commits compared to main yet.
currentBranch.branchNotPushed: The branch has not been pushed to origin yet.

step.noTask: First create a task from this branch or link an existing one.
step.dirty: You have local changes. Commit or stash them before finishing the task.
step.notPushed: Push the branch to origin so others can review it.
step.toReview: You can move the task to review or deploy the branch to DEV.
step.reviewNoDev: Deploy the branch to DEV so a tester can verify the changes.
step.readyMerge: The branch looks ready to merge.
step.highRisk: This branch has elevated risk. Check the changed files before merge.

nav.today: A daily work list: overdue, today's, and upcoming tasks with a date-range picker.
nav.board: Kanban board — your tasks, columns and Git actions.
nav.currentBranch: Everything about the branch you're on and its task.
nav.commandCenter: A lead's view: team, branches, risks, activity and deployments.
nav.branchMap: A visual map of active branches, tasks, risk and merge readiness.
nav.settings: Extension, Git, appearance and sync settings.

git.branch: A separate line of code where you can safely work on one task.
git.push: Pushes the current branch to the Git remote so others can fetch or review it.
git.checkout: Switches the repository to the selected branch.
git.merge: Combines changes from a branch into the main branch.
git.main: The main project branch. Usually only finished and reviewed changes should go here.
git.dirtyTree: You have local changes that have not been committed yet.

ai.copyPrompt: Copies a ready-to-use prompt for Cursor, Claude or Copilot Chat with task details, branch, acceptance criteria and coding rules.
ai.aiAssisted: This task was assisted by AI and should go through the review checklist.
ai.reviewChecklist: A checklist for verifying that AI-assisted changes are safe.

aiAgent.main: An AI agent can prepare local changes on a separate branch. A human still decides about push, DEV and production.
aiAgent.configure: Opens the AI Agent panel to pick an agent, model and personas for this task. Nothing runs automatically.
aiAgent.enable: Enables the AI Agent workflow for this card and stores the prompt, result and run history.
aiAgent.chooseAgent: AGENT = which CLI program does the work (e.g. cursor-agent, claude). Each agent is defined in BranchBoard settings (branchBoard.aiAgents) with its own command, arguments and optional pricing. You can have several agents — e.g. one entry per model, if you want precise per-model cost tracking. Select one or more; the first one checked runs the command.
aiAgent.cursorPersonas: PERSONA = a role/system instruction for the agent (e.g. “senior PHP developer”), loaded from .cursor/agents/*.md files in your repo. A persona does NOT pick the CLI or the model — it only adds a role description, rules and context to the prompt (e.g. “only work on *.php files”). You can attach several at once; ones marked “suggested” match the file types touched by this task.
aiAgent.model: MODEL = the specific LLM engine the agent should use (e.g. Sonnet, Opus, GPT-5). This field is only active when the selected agent has "allowModels": true and a "models" list configured. If your agent is locked to one fixed model per entry (e.g. separate “GPT-5” and “Claude 4 Sonnet” entries), this field is disabled — you choose the model simply by picking the right agent. “auto” leaves the choice to the CLI tool.
aiAgent.modelCustom: Enter the exact model alias supported by the selected agent.
aiAgent.generatePrompt: Builds a prompt from the title, description, checklist, chat, files, task type, branch and project rules.
aiAgent.copyPrompt: Copies the prompt so you can paste it into Cursor, Claude or another tool.
aiAgent.runPlan: Runs the agent in planning mode. It does not edit files and does not move the Kanban card.
aiAgent.runAgent: Creates or checks out the AI branch, runs the agent locally and records changed files. It does not push, merge or deploy.
aiAgent.review: Runs AI review on the current result. This is an assistive check, not a replacement for developer review, and it does not move the card.
aiAgent.moveToColumn: Moves the Kanban card to the AI AGENT column. It does not run the agent or edit files.
aiAgent.stop: Stops the running agent process (SIGTERM). Local changes already made on the branch stay, but further steps will not run.
aiAgent.console: Shows the agent process output (stdout/stderr) live, like a CLI console, while the agent is working.
aiAgent.checkoutBranch: Switches the repository to the branch created for the agent run.
aiAgent.planFile: Opens the markdown plan file saved in the repository under .cursor/plans.
aiAgent.prompt: You can edit the prompt before running. It is stored on the task.
aiAgent.decision: These actions record the human decision about the AI result. They do not push, merge, deploy or automatically move the card between columns.
aiAgent.accept: Marks the AI result as accepted by a human. It is a signal for the next workflow/review step, without Git operations.
aiAgent.reject: Marks the AI result as rejected and leaves the task for follow-up. It does not revert changes automatically.
aiAgent.chat: Each Plan/AI work/Review run is its own "turn" in this history — like a chat with the agent. Review sees the result of the latest AI-work turn. Each turn's prompt is collapsed by default — click to expand it.

aiCostGuard.main: Decides what to send to Cursor CLI — and whether to bother at all — before anything runs. Uses local rules plus an optional local model that only advises; it never edits code, runs Git or launches Cursor CLI itself.
aiCostGuard.check: Analyzes your message and the task's branch/files/chat to suggest an action, context size and an optimized prompt. Nothing runs yet.
aiCostGuard.showPrompt: Shows the optimized prompt that would be sent, so you can review or copy it before running anything.
aiCostGuard.reduceContext: Shrinks the context one step (full → normal → small) and recomputes the decision. Never expands it back automatically.
aiCostGuard.changeModel: Applies the optimizer's suggested model to this task's model picker. You still choose when to run.
aiCostGuard.prepareOnly: Fills the prompt preview below with the optimized prompt without running Cursor CLI.
aiCostGuard.runCursor: Runs the suggested Cursor CLI step (plan/work/review). If the context is full or the estimated cost risk is high, you will be asked to confirm first.

commandCenter.main: A view for a lead or senior developer. It shows team work, branches, risks, activity and merge readiness.
commandCenter.team: Shows team workload to help detect bottlenecks and overloaded people.
commandCenter.activity: A history of changes in tasks, branches, deployments and AI actions.

branchMap.main: The map shows active branches, linked tasks, commits, risks and merge readiness.
branchMap.activeBranch: A branch that has not been closed or merged yet.
branchMap.branchWithoutTask: The branch exists in Git but is not linked to a BranchBoard task.
branchMap.taskWithoutBranch: The task exists but does not have a branch yet.
branchMap.readyToMerge: The branch looks ready to merge, but still requires confirmation.
branchMap.archive: Creates an 'archive/…' tag to preserve the commits, then removes the local branch — it leaves the list but can be restored.
branchMap.deleteLocal: Removes only the local branch. Unmerged work is refused (archive it instead).
branchMap.deleteRemote: Removes the branch from the remote for the whole team. Requires confirmation.

branchFlow.localOnly: This branch exists only on your computer. Others cannot see it until you push it.
branchFlow.remoteOnly: This branch exists on origin but is not checked out locally.
branchFlow.backup: A backup branch created as a safety copy before a risky operation.
branchFlow.stale: A branch with no activity for a while.
branchFlow.cleanup: This branch looks like a cleanup candidate, but the user must confirm the decision.
branchFlow.deleteRemote: Deletes the branch from origin. This affects the whole team and requires confirmation.
branchFlow.createTask: Creates a task linked to this branch and opens it for editing.
branchFlow.linkTask: Links this branch to an existing task that has no branch.
branchFlow.forceDisabled: Force delete is disabled. Archive unmerged branches instead of deleting them.

risk.score: Estimated risk based on changed files, branch age, project areas and testing status.
risk.high: This branch should get a more careful review before merge.
risk.critical: Do not merge without manual review and DEV testing.
risk.criticalPaths: Important project areas such as checkout, payments, database or SEO.

deploy.dev: Deploys the branch to a test environment so changes can be checked before merge.
deploy.openDev: Opens the test environment link for this branch.
deploy.production: A production operation. It requires separate confirmation and should be used carefully.

currentBranch.main: This view shows everything BranchBoard knows about the current branch and its linked task.
currentBranch.aheadBehind: Shows how many commits this branch has ahead of main and how many main commits it does not include yet.
currentBranch.changedFiles: List of files changed on this branch compared to main.
currentBranch.taskFlow: Move the task through work stages. Git operations still require safe confirmation.
currentBranch.transferChanges: Helps move local changes to another branch without running risky commands automatically.
currentBranch.primaryCta: The suggested next action, computed from the branch and task status. Other actions remain available below and in the "More" menu.
currentBranch.moreActions: Other available actions for this branch — nothing was removed, it's just tucked under a menu.
currentBranch.dirtyBlocksPush: This branch has uncommitted changes. Commit or stash them first to continue safely.
currentBranch.aiAgentStage: The stage where an AI agent prepares changes locally. Push, DEV and production still require your decision.
currentBranch.techDetails: Files, commits, safety, discussion and task history — grouped into tabs so they don't crowd the main view.

settings.allowDirectMergeToMain: Allows merging a branch into main from BranchBoard. Recommended only when the team has a clear review process.
settings.requireCleanWorkingTreeBeforeFinish: Prevents finishing a task while you have uncommitted changes. Protects against losing work.
settings.runCommandBeforeFinish: A command (e.g. build/test) run before finishing a task. Finish stops if it fails.
settings.deleteRemoteBranchAfterMerge: Deletes the remote branch after a successful merge to main. Safe only when you no longer work on that branch.
settings.deleteLocalBranchAfterMerge: Deletes the local branch after a successful merge to main.
settings.finishOnMoveToDone: When you move a task with a branch into the DONE column, runs the safe finish flow (push + optional backup and merge to main with confirmation, per your Git policy). Off by default.
settings.storageMode: Local JSON in the project, or a shared SQLite database over SSH for the team.
settings.notifShowToast: Also show a native VS Code notification toast in addition to the bell dropdown.
settings.initials: Shown on your avatar when you don't have a profile photo. Max 2 characters.
settings.aiAgentsModels: For each configured AI agent: the button refreshes its model list from the CLI (if the agent has listModelsArgs set), and the table lets you enter a per-million-token price for each model and turn it on/off. Models with no price are flagged "no price" — BranchBoard never guesses a cost.

aiChat.main: Chat with the AI Agent: one task, one branch, a chosen model and mode. The result always requires your decision (Accept/Reject) and is never automatically pushed, merged, or deployed.
aiChat.modeAgent: Agent: real work on the task's branch, following BranchBoard's safe flow (no auto push/merge/deploy).
aiChat.modePlan: Plan: analysis and a plan of action only — no file changes.
aiChat.modeDebug: Debug: analyzes an error/logs and diagnoses the cause — no file changes.
aiChat.modeMultitask: Multitask: breaks the task into subtasks — no file changes; saving to the checklist requires your confirmation.
aiChat.modeAsk: Ask: a question/answer about the task or code — no file changes.
aiChat.openSettings: Open BranchBoard settings to configure or enable the AI Agent.
aiChat.composer: Enter sends the message, Shift+Enter adds a new line. You can also use /prompt /plan /work /review /rules /diff /save.
aiChat.attach: Insert a task context marker into the message.
aiChat.send: Send the message to the AI Agent in the selected mode.
aiChat.copySolution: Copy the suggested configuration snippet to the clipboard.
aiChat.branchBadge: Branch linked to this task. Green = currently checked out in the repository.
aiChat.agentMenu: Choose which configured AI Agent (CLI) to use for this task.
aiChat.copyFullPrompt: Copy the task's current prompt to the clipboard (generates it first if it doesn't exist yet).
aiChat.saveToTask: Save the latest AI result to the task notes (ai.aiNotes).
aiChat.clearConversation: Clear the visible chat history for this session. Saved runs (Plan/Agent work/Review) remain in the task's history.
aiChat.saveChecklist: Save the latest AI result as task checklist items — requires your confirmation by clicking.

quick.prompt: Generate a prompt from the task context and insert it into the message box.
quick.plan: Insert the /plan command into the message box.
quick.work: Insert the /work command into the message box.
quick.review: Insert the /review command into the message box.
quick.rules: Insert the /rules command (AI Cost Guard check) into the message box.
quick.diff: Insert the /diff command (branch change summary) into the message box.
quick.save: Insert the /save command (save result to task) into the message box.

cc.openHint: Open the CTO view: branch flow, team, risks and activity
cc.openInBrowserHint: Open the dashboard in a separate window (coming soon)
cc.openInBrowserSoon: Opening in a browser will arrive in a later stage.
cc.noRepoHint: Open a folder with a Git repository to see branch flow and risks.

attn.title: Needs attention
attn.emptyHint: No warnings detected for active tasks and branches.
attn.stuck: Task “{title}” has been in one column for over {days} days
attn.noCommits: Branch {branch} has no commits vs main
attn.bigDiff: Branch {branch} differs from main by {count} commits
attn.reviewNoDev: Task “{title}” is in review/testing but has no DEV deploy
attn.branchNoTask: Branch {branch} is not linked to any task
attn.aiFinishedNoReview: AI task “{title}” is finished but has no AI review
attn.aiChangedFilesNoBranch: AI task “{title}” has changed files but no branch
attn.aiStuck: AI task “{title}” is stuck in the AI AGENT column

team.emptyHint: Import users from Git in settings.
team.disclaimer: This view helps detect bottlenecks, not judge people.
team.branchesHint: Number of branches assigned to this person
team.doneThisWeekHint: Tasks completed in the last 7 days

flow.emptyHint: Create a branch from a task or change the filter.

act.emptyHint: Events appear once you start working with tasks and branches.

soon.deploymentsHint: The DEV deployment module arrives in Stage 3.
soon.filesHint: The commit and changed-files browser arrives in Stage 2.
soon.riskHint: Full risk scoring arrives in Stage 2.
soon.aiHint: The AI work dashboard arrives in Stage 2.

risk.emptyHint: Active tasks are below the risk threshold.
risk.note: Rule-based scoring (0–100) from task and branch state. No AI.

r.staleBranch: Branch has not been updated for a while
r.reviewNoDev: In review/testing without a DEV deploy
r.bigNoComments: Large task with no chat discussion
r.aiNoChecklist: AI task without a review checklist

files.emptyHint: Create a branch from a task to browse commits and files.
files.pickHint: Select a branch from the list to see its commits and changed files.

ai.emptyHint: Mark a task as AI-assisted in its details.
ai.note: Keep AI agents in check: review checklists and risk.
ai.noneReady: No AI task is ready yet.

cl.scopeOnly: Are changes limited to this task?
cl.style: Does the code follow project style?
cl.secrets: Are there no secrets or sensitive data?
cl.safeDev: Is it safe to deploy to DEV?

deploy.emptyHint: Deploy a branch to DEV from a task.
deploy.note: What's on each environment: who, when, status, tests and merge-readiness.

aiChat.noActiveModels: No active AI Agent models configured in settings.
aiChat.emptyHint: Generate a prompt from the task context or write your own message to get started.

slash.prompt: Generate a prompt from the task context
slash.work: Run the Agent on the task's branch
slash.rules: Check AI Cost Guard / rules compliance
slash.save: Save the AI result to the task

aiChat.modePromptDebug: DEBUG mode: analyze the error/logs described below, identify the cause and a suggested fix. This is analysis only — do not change any files.
aiChat.modePromptMultitask: MULTITASK mode: break the task below into smaller subtasks/checklist items. This is analysis only — do not change any files. The result is only saved to the task checklist after the user explicitly confirms.
aiChat.modePromptAsk: ASK mode: answer the question below about the task/code. This is analysis only — do not change any files.
aiChat.modePromptPlan: PLAN mode: prepare an implementation plan for the task below. This is analysis only — do not change any files.

error.workspaceTrustDesc: The headless Cursor Agent refused to run because the workspace isn't marked as trusted. BranchBoard never sets this automatically — it requires your explicit consent.
error.workspaceTrustFix: Explicitly add a --trust flag to the agent configuration (never --yolo or -f) and confirm you trust this workspace:

aiChat.noResultToSave: There is no AI result yet to save.
aiChat.savedToTask: Saved the AI result to the task (aiNotes).
aiChat.savedChecklist: Saved {count} items to the task checklist.
aiChat.promptGenerated: Generated a prompt from the task context.
